closeout.no_change_rationale: not_material at Tier-1/CI scale: all three v7 validity gates pass (identity, rep-stability, cross-path storage-equality), but neither B variant clears the 2.0x client-wall decision gate at breadth 50,000 (late_idx 1.39x, late_proj 1.53x). The hydration-stage CPU reduction is real and storage-I/O-isolated (23-29x lower hydration_late.cpu_micros vs hydration_full at byte-identical read_bytes/selected_marks), but dilutes to sub-2x end-to-end wall because hydration is only 57.6% of eager's total query CPU at 50k, with the remaining 42.4% (O(breadth) resolution+samples, byte-identical across paths) unaffected by hydration strategy. The current eager-hydration bound (docs/schemas.md §3.2) plus the existing 100k-stream cap suffice at Tier-1; no §3.2 stage-2 redesign is warranted by this evidence alone. Tier-2 mandate: issue #25 (the 1 TB/7d Tier-2 reference run) must re-evaluate this same decision gate at production scale and file the §3.2 stage-2 redesign product issue then if wall materiality emerges there. See docs/benchmarks/m1-logs-late-hydration.md's Decision section for full detail.
